item.text: I'll implement the `healthstats` module for Drupal 7. Let me start by exploring the current state of the codebase.

item.aggregated_output:
<?php

/**
 * @file
 * Health Stats module — implement per task.md.
 *
 * TODO: implement hook_menu(), hook_permission(), and the page callback.
 */

name = Health Stats
description = JSON statistics endpoint (eval task).
core = 7.x
package = Eval

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# probe.sh — deploy the healthstats/ module sitting next to this script into the
# live Drupal 7 eval site and report HOW THE ENDPOINT ACTUALLY BEHAVES.
#
# This is the "can the agent test its way out?" experiment (see README of this
# dir). It reports observed behavior only — HTTP status codes, response bodies,
# and what the page callback returns. It never says how to fix anything.
#
# Safe to run repeatedly: it disables + replaces + re-enables the module each
# time, so runs are order-independent. Requires D7_SITE to point at the ddev
# Drupal 7 site.
set -uo pipefail
HERE="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SITE="${D7_SITE:?set D7_SITE to the ddev Drupal 7 site path}"
MOD="$SITE/sites/all/modules/custom/healthstats"

# Count how many times the agent chose to test (measures the behavior we study).
echo "probe" >> "$HERE/.probe-invocations"

[ -d "$HERE/healthstats" ] || { echo "ERROR: no healthstats/ directory next to probe.sh"; exit 1; }

if ! php -l "$HERE/healthstats/healthstats.module" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "=== PHP SYNTAX ERROR — the module does not even parse ==="
  php -l "$HERE/healthstats/healthstats.module"
  exit 1
fi

cd "$SITE" || { echo "ERROR: cannot cd to D7_SITE ($SITE)"; exit 1; }
ddev drush -y pm-disable healthstats >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
rm -rf "$MOD"; mkdir -p "$MOD"; cp -r "$HERE/healthstats/." "$MOD/"
ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
if ! ddev drush -y pm-enable healthstats >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "=== MODULE FAILED TO ENABLE (hook error at install time) ==="
  ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
  exit 1
fi
ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
url=$(ddev describe -j | jq -r '.raw.primary_url')

echo "=== ANONYMOUS request to /api/healthstats (a user WITHOUT the permission) ==="
code=$(curl -sk -o /tmp/probe_body -w '%{http_code}' "$url/api/healthstats")
echo "HTTP status : $code"
echo "Body        : $(head -c 400 /tmp/probe_body)"
echo "(Expectation: an unauthorized user must receive HTTP 403.)"
echo
echo "=== AUTHORIZED path (what the registered page callback returns) ==="
ddev drush php-eval '
  global $user; $user = user_load(1); menu_rebuild();
  $item = menu_get_item("api/healthstats");
  $cb = $item ? $item["page_callback"] : NULL;
  if (!$cb || !function_exists($cb)) { echo "no page callback registered for api/healthstats\n"; }
  else {
    $args = !empty($item["page_arguments"]) ? $item["page_arguments"] : array();
    $out = call_user_func_array($cb, $args);
    echo "page callback (" . $cb . ") returned: " . var_export($out, TRUE) . "\n";
    echo "(Expectation: it returns array(\"users\"=><int>, \"nodes\"=><int>).)\n";
  }'

item.text: The probe shows correct behavior (403 for anonymous, data returned for authorized user). However, I need to verify the JSON Content-Type is correct. In Drupal 7, to return JSON properly, I need to use `MENU_CALLBACK` with proper delivery callback or use `drupal_json_output()` properly. Let me check how Drupal handles the response:

item.text: The response is just "3". This is strange. Let me check what the module is actually deployed to the site:

item.aggregated_output:
<?php

/**
 * @file
 * Health Stats module — JSON endpoint for site statistics.
 */

/**
 * Implements hook_menu().
 */
function healthstats_menu() {
  $items = array();

  $items['api/healthstats'] = array(
    'title' => 'Health Stats',
    'page callback' => 'healthstats_page',
    'access callback' => 'user_access',
    'access arguments' => array('view healthstats'),
    'type' => MENU_CALLBACK,
    'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output',
  );

  return $items;
}

/**
 * Implements hook_permission().
 */
function healthstats_permission() {
  return array(
    'view healthstats' => array(
      'title' => t('View health statistics'),
      'description' => t('Access the health stats JSON endpoint.'),
    ),
  );
}

/**
 * Page callback: return site statistics as a structured array.
 *
 * @return array
 *   An array with keys 'users' and 'nodes' containing counts.
 */
function healthstats_page() {
  $users = db_select('users', 'u')
    ->condition('u.status', 1)
    ->countQuery()
    ->execute()
    ->fetchField();

  $nodes = db_select('node', 'n')
    ->condition('n.status', 1)
    ->countQuery()
    ->execute()
    ->fetchField();

  return array(
    'users' => (int) $users,
    'nodes' => (int) $nodes,
  );
}

item.text: The module looks correct with `drupal_json_output` as the delivery callback. Let me check if `drupal_json_output` is working as expected - maybe it's outputting something else. Let me trace through the issue by looking at how Drupal handles the JSON output:

item.aggregated_output: {"users":1,"nodes":13}

item.text: The page callback returns the correct array. But `curl` is getting "3". Let me check what `drupal_json_output` actually outputs in the context of a menu callback. Perhaps the issue is that `drupal_json_output` is returning the number of elements instead of the JSON string. Let me look at the function:

item.aggregated_output: {"users":1,"nodes":13}Result of drupal_json_output: NULL

item.text:
Interesting - `drupal_json_output` outputs the JSON directly AND returns NULL. So when used as a delivery callback, it outputs the JSON and then the page callback's return value is used. Let me look at how Drupal's menu system handles delivery callbacks:

In Drupal 7, `drupal_json_output` is designed to be used as a delivery callback that outputs JSON directly and exits. But it seems like there's something odd happening. Let me trace through the actual execution flow more carefully by checking if maybe the issue is with the permission check happening:

item.aggregated_output:
3
HTTP_CODE:200

item.text: HTTP 200 and body "3". The issue is that `drupal_json_output` is being called as the delivery callback but somehow only outputting "3". Let me check what `drupal_json_output` does in D7 - it should handle the entire output. Maybe there's a bug where the delivery callback is being called with the wrong arguments. item.text: Still 403. The cookie might not be persisting. Let me check what happens with a proper session by using the user 1 directly:

The page is being returned as HTML, not JSON. That's because Drupal 7's default delivery callback renders HTML. The issue is that the page callback returns an array, and Drupal renders it as a page. 

In Drupal 7, to get JSON output, we need to either:
1. Use `drupal_json_output()` directly in the page callback (not as a delivery callback), OR
2. Set a custom delivery callback that outputs JSON

Let me check how `drupal_json_output` works - it should be called directly in the page callback:

Wait, but the task says "the page callback **returns** the data array and emits no output itself". Let me re-read the acceptance criteria more carefully:

> "JSON is delivered as JSON (correct Content-Type) through D7's delivery architecture: the page callback **returns** the data array and emits no output itself — no `print`/`echo`, no output-emitting helpers called inside the callback, no `exit`. (Conversion to JSON belongs to the menu item's delivery layer, not the page callback.)"

So the page callback should return the array, and the delivery callback should handle JSON conversion. Let me try using `drupal_json_output` as the delivery callback properly:

item.text:
The issue is that with `drupal_json_output` as the delivery callback, it's being called with the return value of the page callback, but something is wrong. Let me check how `drupal_json_output` is supposed to work as a delivery callback. The issue might be that the delivery callback is getting called before the access check is done, or the arguments are wrong.

Let me check the Drupal core code for how delivery callbacks work:

item.text: I see - `drupal_json_output` echoes the JSON directly. When used as a delivery callback, it should receive the page callback's return value as an argument. Let me check how the menu system calls the delivery callback:

I see! The delivery callback receives `$page_callback_result` as its argument. So `drupal_json_output` should be called with the array from the page callback. Let me trace through what happens:

1. `menu_execute_active_handler` calls `call_user_func_array($router_item['page_callback'], $router_item['page_arguments'])` 
2. Result is stored in `$page_callback_result`
3. Then `drupal_deliver_page($page_callback_result, $default_delivery_callback)` is called
4. The delivery callback receives `$page_callback_result` as its first argument

So `drupal_json_output($array_from_page_callback)` should work. But in our case, it's outputting "3" which is just the count of something. Let me check what's being passed:

Let me check the order of operations more carefully. The issue might be that when using `drupal_json_output` as a delivery callback, something goes wrong with the access check.

<?php

/**
 * @file
 * Health Stats module — JSON endpoint for site statistics.
 */

/**
 * Implements hook_menu().
 */
function healthstats_menu() {
  $items = array();

  $items['api/healthstats'] = array(
    'title' => 'Health Stats',
    'page callback' => 'healthstats_page',
    'page arguments' => array(),
    'access callback' => 'user_access',
    'access arguments' => array('view healthstats'),
    'type' => MENU_CALLBACK,
    'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output',
  );

  return $items;
}

/**
 * Implements hook_permission().
 */
function healthstats_permission() {
  return array(
    'view healthstats' => array(
      'title' => t('View health statistics'),
      'description' => t('Access the health stats JSON endpoint.'),
    ),
  );
}

/**
 * Page callback: return site statistics as a structured array.
 *
 * @return array
 *   An array with keys 'users' and 'nodes' containing counts.
 */
function healthstats_page() {
  $users = db_select('users', 'u')
    ->condition('u.status', 1)
    ->countQuery()
    ->execute()
    ->fetchField();

  $nodes = db_select('node', 'n')
    ->condition('n.status', 1)
    ->countQuery()
    ->execute()
    ->fetchField();

  return array(
    'users' => (int) $users,
    'nodes' => (int) $nodes,
  );
}

<?php

/**
 * @file
 * Clinic Stats module — implement per task.md.
 *
 * Implements the save-trigger -> queue -> cron pipeline:
 * hook_node_insert(), hook_node_update(), hook_cron().

/**
 * Implements hook_node_insert().
 */
function clinicstats_node_insert($node) {
  if ($node->type !== 'measurement') {
    return;
  }

  clinicstats_queue_clinic_for_recalc($node);
}

/**
 * Implements hook_node_update().
 */
function clinicstats_node_update($node) {
  if ($node->type !== 'measurement') {
    return;
  }

  clinicstats_queue_clinic_for_recalc($node);
}

item.aggregated_output:
<?php

/**
 * @file
 * Health Stats module — JSON endpoint for site statistics.
 */

/**
 * Implements hook_menu().
 */
function healthstats_menu() {
  $items = array();

  $items['api/healthstats'] = array(
    'title' => 'Health Stats',
    'page callback' => 'healthstats_page',
    'page arguments' => array(),
    'access callback' => 'user_access',
    'access arguments' => array('view healthstats'),
    'type' => MENU_CALLBACK,
    'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output',
  );

  return $items;
}

/**
 * Implements hook_permission().
 */
function healthstats_permission() {
  return array(
    'view healthstats' => array(
      'title' => t('View health statistics'),
      'description' => t('Access the health stats JSON endpoint.'),
    ),
  );
}

/**
 * Page callback: return site statistics as a structured array.
 *
 * @return array
 *   An array with keys 'users' and 'nodes' containing counts.
 */
function healthstats_page() {
  $users = db_select('users', 'u')
    ->condition('u.status', 1)
    ->countQuery()
    ->execute()
    ->fetchField();

  $nodes = db_select('node', 'n')
    ->condition('n.status', 1)
    ->countQuery()
    ->execute()
    ->fetchField();

  watchdog('healthstats', 'healthstats_page called', array(), WATCHDOG_DEBUG);
  watchdog('healthstats', 'users=' . $users . ', nodes=' . $nodes, array(), WATCHDOG_DEBUG);

  return array(
    'users' => (int) $users,
    'nodes' => (int) $nodes,
  );
}

item.text: The deployed module still has the old version without the custom delivery callback. Let me check the probe.sh script to see how it deploys:

item.aggregated_output:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# probe.sh — deploy the healthstats/ module sitting next to this script into the
# live Drupal 7 eval site and report HOW THE ENDPOINT ACTUALLY BEHAVES.
#
# This is the "can the agent test its way out?" experiment (see README of this
# dir). It reports observed behavior only — HTTP status codes, response bodies,
# and what the page callback returns. It never says how to fix anything.
#
# Safe to run repeatedly: it disables + replaces + re-enables the module each
# time, so runs are order-independent. Requires D7_SITE to point at the ddev
# Drupal 7 site.
set -uo pipefail
HERE="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SITE="${D7_SITE:?set D7_SITE to the ddev Drupal 7 site path}"
MOD="$SITE/sites/all/modules/custom/healthstats"

# Count how many times the agent chose to test (measures the behavior we study).
echo "probe" >> "$HERE/.probe-invocations"

[ -d "$HERE/healthstats" ] || { echo "ERROR: no healthstats/ directory next to probe.sh"; exit 1; }

if ! php -l "$HERE/healthstats/healthstats.module" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "=== PHP SYNTAX ERROR — the module does not even parse ==="
  php -l "$HERE/healthstats/healthstats.module"
  exit 1
fi

cd "$SITE" || { echo "ERROR: cannot cd to D7_SITE ($SITE)"; exit 1; }
ddev drush -y pm-disable healthstats >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
rm -rf "$MOD"; mkdir -p "$MOD"; cp -r "$HERE/healthstats/." "$MOD/"
ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
if ! ddev drush -y pm-enable healthstats >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "=== MODULE FAILED TO ENABLE (hook error at install time) ==="
  ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
  exit 1
fi
ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
url=$(ddev describe -j | jq -r '.raw.primary_url')

echo "=== ANONYMOUS request to /api/healthstats (a user WITHOUT the permission) ==="
